source_metadata.title_raw: Jason R. Alcala - A-18-036 - April 17, 2018 - Livermore

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
, FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1 1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) :i :!:.! -5660 • Fax (916) :i:Z:!•0886 
Jason R. Alcala 
Livennore City Attorney 
City Hall 
1052 South Livennore A venue 
Livennore, CA 94550 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-036 
Dear Mr. Alcala: 
April 17, 2018 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Livennore Airport 
Commissioner Alex Shezifi regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Refonn Act 
(the "Act")1 and Government Code Section 1090. Please note that we are only providing advice 
under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act, and Section 1090, not under other general 
conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest, including Public Contract 
Code. Also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
In regard to our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all 
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the Alameda County 
District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097 .1 ( c )(3 ). ) We did not receive a 
written response from either entity. (Section 1097.l(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that, 
for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding 
against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.l(c)(S).) 
QUESTION 
1. Under the Act and Section 1090, may Commissioner Shezifi take part in the Airport 
Commission's consideration of Attitude Aviation's request to recommend that the City 
Council revise the Minimum Standards for Commercial Aeronautical Activities for 
Livennore Airport, given that Commissioner Shezifi's spouse is employed by Attitude 
Aviation? 
2. Does Section 1090 preclude the entire Airport Commission from considering Attitude 
Aviation's request to recommend that the City Council revise the Minimum Standards for 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-18-036 
Page No. 2 
Commercial Aeronautical Activities for Livennore Airport, given that Commissioner 
Shezifi's spouse is an Attitude Aviation employee? 
CONCLUSION 
Under the Act, Commissioner Shezifi may not take part in the Airport Commission's 
consideration of Attitude Aviation's request because Commissioner Shezifi has a disqualifying 
conflict ofinterest in his spouse's source-of-income interest in Attitude Aviation. However, Section 
1090 does not apply as the request does not involve a "contract." 
FACTS AS PROVIDED BY THE REQUESTOR 
The Livennore Municipal Airport (the "Airport") is located in the City of Livennore and is 
operated by the City as an enterprise. The Airport is a general aviation reliever airport that serves 
private, business, and corporate tenants and customers. 
The City Council established the Airport Commission as an advisory body to the City 
Council, Planning Commission, and City staff- including the Airport Manager. The Airport 
Commission provides advice concerning the Airport, the aviation industry, and "operational 
policies and regulations pertaining to tenants and uses of the airport." Membership, duties, and 
responsibilities of the Airport Commission are set forth in Chapter 2.41 of the Livennore Municipal 
Code. In addition to its advisory role, the Airport Commission has regulatory responsibilities that 
include: amending and approving the large hangar waitlist policy, conducting appeal hearings, and 
making detenninations on those hearings, which include the appeal of any order or directive of the 
Airport Manager. (See Livennore Municipal Code Chapters 2.41.020(F) and 11.08.190.) 
On October, 24, 2016, Alex Shezifi was re-appointed to the Airport Commission. He was 
re-elected as the Commission's Chairperson on February 12, 2018. Commissioner Shezifi
1s spouse 
is employed by Attitude Aviation and her income from Attitude Aviation aggregates to more than 
$500 in the previous 12 months. 
Specialized aviation service operators engaged in commercial aeronautical activities at the 
Airport are required to obtain commercial aviation pennits from the Airport Manager. Attitude 
Aviation maintains a commercial presence at the Airport, where it offers flight instruction, and has 
a commercial aviation pennit for ground instruction and recreational flying. 
Attitude Aviation requested a self-fueling pennit from the Airport Manager that specifies a 
fuel storage tank with a capacity smaller than the minimum size specified in the Airport 
Commission's Minimum Standards for Commercial Aeronautical Activities (the "Minimum 
Standards"). The Airport Manager declined the request on the grounds that the smaller tank did not 
comply with the requirements for a Self-Fueling Operator in the Minimum Standards. 
Following the Airport Manager's decision, Attitude Aviation appeared before the Airport 
Commission at a regularly noticed meeting and requested the placement of an item on a future 
agenda to discuss making a recommendation to the City Council to revise the self-fueling 
requirements in the Minimum Standards to allow Attitude Aviation to obtain a self-fueling permit 


File No. A-18-036 
Page No. 3 
with a smaller capacity fuel storage tank. During that same meeting, the Airport Commission 
directed staff to add the item to a future agenda 
At the time of this request, City staff is preparing a staff report to present to the Airport 
Commission at a future regularly noticed meeting to consider whether to recommend that City 
Council revise the self-fueling requirements in the Minimum Standards as requested by Attitude 
Aviation. 
Due to his financial interest in Attitude Aviation based upon his spouse's income, 
Commissioner Shezifi intends to recuse himself from participation when the Airport Commission 
considers the request from Attitude Aviation. Nevertheless, he seeks advice as to whether that 
action is appropriate and whether there are any additional conflicts to consider under Section 1090 
that might affect his participation, or the Airport Commission's participation, due to his financial 
interest in Attitude Aviation. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial 
interest. 2 Section 87103 provides that a public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental 
decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on the 
official or his or her spouse. Specific to your facts, Section 87103 sets forth the interests from which 
a conflict of interest may arise, including: 
• 
An interest in any source of income aggregating $500 or more to the official within 12 
months prior to the decision. (Section 871~3(c).) 
• 
The official's interest in his or her personal finances and those ofimmediate family 
members. (Section 87103; Regulation 18702.5.) An official always has an interest in his 
or her personal finances. 
You stated that Commissioner Shezifi's wife will receive income aggregating more than 
$500 from Attitude Aviation in the 12 months prior to the decision. The.Act's definition of 
"income" includes any community property interest in a spouse's income. (Section 82030(a).) 
Therefore, Commissioner Shezifi has a financial interest in his wife's source of income and the 
personal finances of his wife. 
2 Based upon the facts provided, the Airport Commission has regulatory responsibilities that include: amending 
and approving the large hangar waitlist policy, conducting appeal hearings, and making determinations on those 
hearings. Accordingly, Commissioner Shezifi is a public official subject to the Act's conflict of interest provisions. (See 
Regulation 18700(c)(I).) 


Foreseeability and Materiality 
File No. A-18-036 
Page No. 4 
Standards for foreseeability vary dep~nding on whether an interest is explicitly involved in a 
governmental decision. An official's financial interest is explicitly involved in a decision, and is 
presumed to be reasonably foreseeable, if the interest is a named party in, or the subject of, a 
decision before the official or the official's agency. (Regulation 18701(a}.} A financial interest is 
the subject of a proceeding if the decision involves the issuance, renewal, approval, denial, or 
revocation of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the financial interest. For 
a financial interest that is not explicitly involved in the decision at issue, the financial effect of the 
decision on an official's interest is reasonably foreseeable ifit can be recognized as a realistic 
possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical. (Regulation 18701 (b ). ) 
In determining whether a business is the subject of a decision involving a generally applied 
ordinance, we must examine the scope of the ordinance. For instance, we have previously advised 
that an official's vineyard was not the subject of a decision to adopt a proposed ordinance 
regulating the operation of wineries within Sonoma County, including placing certain limitations on 
the number of events that could be held at the wineries, because the ordinance affected all wineries 
County-wide. (Cooke Advice Letter, No. A-99-127.) However, in this case, Attitude Aviation's 
specifically requested that the Airport Commission recommend revisions to the Minimum 
Standards in an effort to obtain a self-fueling permit. Additionally, the Minimum Standards apply 
narrowly and only to Attitude Aviation or other business seeking a self-fueling permit with a 
smaller capacity storage tank. In this case, Commissioner Shezifi's financial interest in Attitude 
Aviation is explicitly involved in the decision at issue. 
Regulation 18702.3 provides the materiality standards for sources of income. For salary 
received from a business entity, the financial effect of a governmental decision is material if the 
business entity will be financially effected under the standards applied in Regulation 18702.1. 
Under Regulation 18702.1 (a), the reasonably foreseeable financial effect of a decision on a business 
entity in which an official has a financial interest is material whenever the business entity: 
· 
"(I} Initiates the proceeding in which the governmental decision will be 
made by filing an application, claim, appeal, or request for other government action 
concerning the business entity; 
[,r] ... [11 
"(7) Is otherwise subject to an action taken by the official's agency, the effect 
of which is directed solely at the business entity in which the official has an 
interest." 
Under the facts provided, Attitude Aviation initiated the Airport Commission's decision by 
requesting a recommendation to revise the Minimum Standards to allow the use of a smaller 
capacity tank. Moreover, Attitude Aviation is "otherwise subject to" the Airport Commission's 
recommendation to the City Council, the effect of which would be directed solely at Attitude 
Aviation, in that revision to the Minimum Standards would allow Attitude A via ti on to acquire a 


File No. A-18-036 
Page No. 5 
self-fueling pennit with a smaller fuel tank. Therefore, the financial effect is material under 
Regulation 18702.1 (a)(l) and (a)(7). 
· Under the Act, Commissioner Shezifi has a disqualifying conflict of ~nterest and he must 
recuse himself from taking part in the Airport Commission's decision to recommend a revision of 
the Minimum Standards pursuant to Regulation 18707.3 
Section 1090 
Section 1090 generally prohibits public officers, while acting in their official capacities, 
from making contracts in which they are financially interested. A contract that violates Section 
1090 is void. (Thomson v. Call (1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 646.) The prohibition applies regardless of 
whether the tenns of the contract are fair and equitable to all parties. (Id. at pp. 646-649.) 
For Section 1090 to apply, the matter at issue must involve a contract. Traditional contract 
principles are applied to detennine whether a contract is involved for purposes of Section 1090. 
However, both the Attorney General's Office and the Commission have previously detennined that 
a modification of a regulatory pennit is a regulatory matter that does not involve a "contract" for 
purposes of Section 1090.4 
Similarly, revising the self-fueling requirements in the Minimum Standards to allow 
Attitude Aviation to obtain a self-fueling pennit with a smaller capacity fuel storage tank is a 
modification of a regulatory pennit and not a contract for purposes of Section 1090. Accordingly, 
Section 1090 is inapplicable and does not preclude the remaining members of the Airport 
· 
Commission from taking part in such a decision. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
SKP:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Brian G. Lau 
3 A public official who holds an office specified in Section 87200 (including city councilmembers) and who 
has a disqualifying conflict of interest in a decision noticed at a public meeting must: ·(I) publicly identify each financial 
interest that gives rise to the disqualifying conflict of interest, (2) recuse himself or herself from the from the decision, 
and (3) leave the room for the duration of the discussion and/or vote on the item. (Section 87105; Regulation 18707.) 
229. 
4 84 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 34 (2001); Ca111al/10 Advice Letter, No. A-16-265; Mi/le,- Advice Leiter, No. A- 15-

interest in Attitude Aviation.
